headline: AWK Concludes the Acquisition of Nexus Utility Assets for $315 Million
summary: American Water Works closes a $315M Nexus deal across eight states, adding 47,000 customers as it pushes acquisitions and a $3.7B 2026 capex plan.

raw.headline: AWK Concludes the Acquisition of Nexus Utility Assets for $315 Million

headline: American Water Joins Dialogue on Affordability at the Western Conference of Public Service Commissioners 2026 Annual Meeting
summary: American Water (NYSE: AWK), the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the U.S., joins important conversations during the Western Conference of Public Service Commissioners (WCPSC) 2026 Annual Meeting, held May 31 through June 3, 2026, in Santa Ana Pueblo, N.M. WCPSC is a regional association within the National Association of Regulatory Utility Commissioners (NARUC) and brings together commissioners, regulators, utilities and industry leaders.

headline: Assessing American Water Works Company (AWK) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
summary: American Water Works Company (AWK) stock moves after recent performance American Water Works Company (AWK) has drawn attention after a period of weaker share performance, with the stock down 5% over the past month and 11% over the past 3 months. Against that backdrop, investors are weighing its US$24.1b market value, recent annual revenue of US$5.2b and net income of US$1.1b, alongside a value score of 1 and longer term total return declines. See our latest analysis for American Water Works...
